methodology_note: harm_ly and surplus_ly represent approximate person-years of harm or benefit. Core formula: deaths × avg_remaining_life_years (~38 yrs for mixed civilian/combatant population); serious injuries × years_of_disability (3-10 yrs depending on severity); displacement × years_displaced × quality_factor (0.3-0.5); systemic harms = affected_population × years × quality_reduction_fraction. harm_ecy = harm_ly × 0.63 (economic-cost equivalent, consistent with prior series entries). All values rounded to nearest thousand. Entries marked DISCRETE cover bounded military operations; entries marked SYSTEMIC cover ongoing policy harms. Some overlap may exist between occupation (isr_harm_001) and specific systemic entries (020-021); those entries isolate documented sub-harms for auditability.

entries.description: ICJ Advisory Opinion: Israel's occupation of Palestinian territory including East Jerusalem is unlawful; breaches prohibition on racial discrimination and apartheid; must end occupation, dismantle all settlements, provide reparations and allow return of displaced Palestinians
entries.causation_evidence: International Court of Justice Advisory Opinion on Legal Consequences of Israel's Policies in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (19 July 2024)

entries.description: Gaza military campaign post-Oct 7 2023: over 45,000 Palestinians killed (majority civilians, women and children), 69% of structures destroyed or damaged, widespread famine and water deprivation amounting to acts of genocide per HRW and Amnesty assessments
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA Gaza updates, HRW 'Extermination and Acts of Genocide' report (Dec 2024), Amnesty International genocide assessment (Dec 2024)

entries.description: Forced displacement and demolition in West Bank refugee camps (Jenin, Tulkarm, Nur Shams): tens of thousands displaced, neighborhoods systematically destroyed during 'Iron Wall' operation; ethnic cleansing concerns raised by UN
entries.causation_evidence: HRW 'All My Dreams Have Been Erased' report (Nov 2025) and UN human rights office statements

entries.description: Record settlement expansion: over 20,000 new housing units approved since 2023 plus 43 new settlements; ICJ-mandated dismantling ignored
entries.causation_evidence: ICJ Advisory Opinion, Peace Now tracking, UN Human Rights Council settlement reports

entries.description: Over 860 violent settler attacks in West Bank in 2025 alone; systematic failure to protect Palestinian civilians or prosecute offenders
entries.causation_evidence: Amnesty International tracking and UN OCHA reports

entries.description: ICC arrest warrants issued for Prime Minister Netanyahu and Defense Minister Gallant for war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza
entries.causation_evidence: International Criminal Court statements (Nov 2024)

entries.description: Ongoing post-ceasefire attacks and denial of humanitarian aid in Gaza despite ICJ orders; continued bombardment and restrictions leading to famine conditions
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA updates and HRW reports post-October 2025 ceasefire

entries.description: ICJ-ordered end to occupation and settlement regime ignored; escalation of attacks on civilians and natural resources in Gaza and West Bank post-ruling
entries.causation_evidence: OHCHR expert statements and UN reports on non-compliance with ICJ Advisory Opinion

entries.description: Use of white phosphorus in Gaza and Lebanon operations; documented civilian harm and environmental damage
entries.causation_evidence: HRW and Amnesty International Crisis Evidence Lab reports

entries.description: Mass destruction of housing and civilian infrastructure in Gaza ('domicide'); 69% of structures damaged or destroyed rendering areas uninhabitable
entries.causation_evidence: UN HRC Domicide Report (Feb 2026) and UNOSAT satellite analysis

entries.description: Arbitrary detention and ill-treatment of thousands of Palestinians amounting to torture; ignored ICJ instructions on humanitarian access
entries.causation_evidence: Amnesty International annual report and UN findings

entries.description: Continued West Bank settlement approvals including legalization of 22 outposts and E1 expansion; burying prospects for Palestinian statehood
entries.causation_evidence: Peace Now and UN settlement reports (2025)

entries.description: Ongoing failure to investigate or prosecute alleged war crimes and violations during Gaza operations despite internal and international calls
entries.causation_evidence: Israeli NGO platform reports and UN Commission of Inquiry findings

entries.description: Operation Cast Lead (Dec 2008 - Jan 2009): 1,383-1,417 Palestinians killed (majority civilians, 308-333 children); 5,300+ wounded (30% with long-term impairment); 20,000+ made homeless; 3,540 housing units completely destroyed. Harm_ly: deaths ~1,400 × 38 yrs = 53,200; long-term injuries 1,590 × 5 = 7,950; short-term injuries 3,710 × 1 = 3,710; displacement 20,000 × 3 yrs = 60,000; infrastructure loss 3,540 homes × 5.5 persons × 2 yrs = 39,000; white phosphorus documented civilian harm ~15,000; total ~179,000.
entries.causation_evidence: UN OCHA 'Eight years after Cast Lead' report (2017); B'Tselem casualty database; Amnesty International 'Operation Cast Lead: 22 Days of Death and Destruction' (2009); Goldstone Report (UN Fact Finding Mission, Sep 2009)

entries.description: Operation Pillar of Defense (Nov 2012, 8 days): 174 Palestinians killed (133 civilians, 41 combatants per PCHR); 1,269 wounded; ~10,000 briefly displaced; infrastructure damage across Gaza. Harm_ly: deaths 174 × 38 = 6,612; wounds 1,269 × 2 = 2,538; displacement 10,000 × 0.3 = 3,000; infrastructure ~3,000; total ~15,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Palestinian Centre for Human Rights (PCHR) comprehensive report on Pillar of Defense (2013); UN OCHA situation reports Nov 2012; B'Tselem casualty database

entries.description: Operation Protective Edge (Jul-Aug 2014, 50 days): 2,251 Palestinians killed (B'Tselem: 1,462 civilians incl. 526 non-combatant minors); 11,200+ wounded (1,000 children left permanently disabled); 500,000 displaced at peak; 108,000 rendered permanently homeless; 18,000 housing units destroyed or severely damaged; 73 schools and 24 medical facilities damaged. Harm_ly: deaths 2,251 × 38 = 85,538; permanent disability 1,000 × 10 = 10,000; other injuries 10,200 × 2 = 20,400; permanent homelessness 108,000 × 3 yrs = 324,000; temporary displacement 400,000 × 0.5 = 200,000; total ~640,000.
entries.causation_evidence: B'Tselem '50 Days: More than 500 Children' report (2016); UN OCHA OPCA 2014 war report; IMEU statistical analysis; B'Tselem '4.5 Years After: 13,000 Gazans Still Homeless' (2019)

entries.description: Operation Guardian of the Walls (May 2021, 11 days): 232 Palestinians killed in Gaza (B'Tselem: 129 civilians incl. 67 children); ~1,900 wounded; 74,000 displaced; significant residential and infrastructure damage. Harm_ly: deaths 232 × 38 = 8,816; wounds 1,900 × 2 = 3,800; displacement 74,000 × 0.3 = 22,200; infrastructure ~5,000; total ~40,000.
entries.causation_evidence: B'Tselem casualty database; UN OCHA Flash Update May 2021; Al Mezan Center for Human Rights report (2021)

entries.description: Gaza land, air, and sea blockade 2007-2023 (16 years): 2.1M people under comprehensive closure. UNCTAD: real GDP per capita fell 27% (2006-2022); 63% food insecure per UNRWA; average unemployment 40%+; 90%+ of aquifer water unfit for drinking; Physicians for Human Rights Israel documented 1,000+ deaths from denied medical exit permits. Harm_ly: 2.1M people × 16 yrs × 0.04 quality reduction (poverty, unemployment, food insecurity, healthcare deprivation) = 1,344,000; medical exit mortality 1,000 × 30 = 30,000; child stunting/malnutrition long-term ~100,000 children × 3 yrs impairment = 300,000; overlap-adjusted and rounded to 1,800,000. Note: does not double-count casualties from discrete military operations (isr_harm_014, 015, 016, 017).
entries.causation_evidence: UNCTAD 'Economic Impact of the Destruction in Gaza' (2024); UNRWA food security reports 2007-2023; WHO EMRO report on food insecurity and malnutrition in Gaza Strip (2025); Physicians for Human Rights Israel denied medical exit permit documentation; OCHA 'Fragmented Lives' series

entries.description: 2006 Lebanon War (33 days): 1,191 Lebanese killed per Lebanon Higher Relief Council (HRW: majority civilians; 302 of 499 confirmed were women or children); 4,409 wounded (15% permanently disabled = 661 people); 1M+ displaced (~25% of Lebanese population); 15,000+ homes destroyed; HRW: Israel fired 4.6 million cluster submunitions, 90% in final 3 days before ceasefire. Post-war cluster casualties tracked separately (isr_harm_025). Harm_ly: deaths 1,191 × 38 = 45,258; permanent injuries 661 × 7 = 4,627; other injuries 3,748 × 2 = 7,496; displacement 1,000,000 × 0.5 = 500,000; infrastructure (15,000 homes × 5 persons × 1.5 yrs = 112,500); adjusted total ~700,000.
entries.causation_evidence: HRW 'Why They Died: Civilian Casualties in Lebanon during the 2006 War' (2007/2008); Lebanon Higher Relief Council death toll; Frontiers journal 'Characteristics of injuries during the 2006 Lebanon conflict' (2024); UN SC S/2006/670

entries.description: West Bank movement restrictions (ongoing since 2002 peak): 700+ movement obstacles including checkpoints, earth mounds, roadblocks, settler-only roads affecting 3M Palestinians. World Bank 2013: restrictions cost ~35% of potential GDP. Healthcare delays documented; OCHA tracks regular deaths at checkpoints. Harm_ly: 3M people × 23 yrs (2002-2025) × 0.015 quality reduction (economic loss, healthcare delays, daily obstruction) = 1,035,000; rounded to 1,000,000. Note: partially overlaps with isr_harm_001; this entry isolates the documented sub-harm of physical movement infrastructure.
entries.causation_evidence: World Bank 'Fiscal Developments and Macroeconomic Performance' West Bank 2013 (35% GDP cost estimate); OCHA 'Movement and Access in the West Bank' annual reports; B'Tselem checkpoint database; WHO/OCHA documented checkpoint deaths

entries.description: West Bank water access discrimination (since 1967, Israel seized control of all water resources): Palestinians consume 82.4 L/person/day (2020); Israelis 247 L/day (3x ratio); off-grid communities: 26 L/day vs WHO minimum 50-100 L/day; Israelis consume 10x Palestinians' total despite being 3x the population. 100,000+ Palestinians not connected to water grid. Harm_ly: 3M Palestinians × 30 yrs (1995-2025 post-Oslo period) × 0.005 quality reduction from health/agricultural impact of systematic under-allocation = 450,000; excess disease burden from contaminated/insufficient water ~50,000; rounded to 400,000.
entries.causation_evidence: B'Tselem 'Parched: Israel's Policy of Water Deprivation in the West Bank' (May 2023); Amnesty International 'The Occupation of Water' (2017); Frontiers in Water 'Water at the Intersection of Human Rights and Conflict: A Case Study of Palestine' (2024); B'Tselem 'Thirsty for a Solution' report

entries.description: Home demolitions in West Bank Area C and East Jerusalem (pre-2023): OCHA documented 700-900 demolitions per year in Area C in recent years; B'Tselem documented 8,000+ total structures demolished 2006-2022; average household size 5.5 people. Each demolition typically also destroys water cisterns, solar panels, livestock structures. Harm_ly: 8,000 demolitions × 5.5 persons = 44,000 directly displaced; × 2 yrs to resettle = 88,000 person-years; plus trauma, lost generational assets, repeated displacement multiplier ~1.7x = 150,000.
entries.causation_evidence: B'Tselem 'Demolitions and Forced Displacement' database; OCHA 'West Bank Demolition and Displacement' monthly updates; UN OCHA Humanitarian Overview 2022

entries.description: Administrative detention and documented torture of Palestinians (historical pattern, focused 2000-2025): UN Special Rapporteur on Torture 2024 documented systematic ill-treatment and torture during 2023-2024 Gaza operations; peak Oct 2023: ~9,000 held in administrative detention simultaneously; B'Tselem: estimated 850,000+ total Palestinian detentions since 1967. Focused 2000-2025 period: approximately 50,000+ individual administrative detention episodes averaging 8-10 months = ~37,500 person-years directly incarcerated; torture severity multiplier and multi-year psychological aftermath bring to ~90,000; family members affected (4 per detainee × 50,000 × 0.2 quality × 0.5 yrs) ~20,000; infrastructure of fear across 3M West Bank population ~40,000; total ~150,000.
entries.causation_evidence: UN Special Rapporteur on Torture report to General Assembly (2024); B'Tselem 'Without Trial: Administrative Detention of Palestinians by Israel' report; HaMoked detention data; Amnesty International annual reports 2000-2025

entries.description: Attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Gaza (Oct 2023 - Dec 2024): WHO documented 654 attacks on healthcare; all 36 hospitals damaged; only 16 partially functional by mid-2024; 2.3M people without adequate healthcare access. Preventable deaths from treatable conditions (renal failure, cancer, obstetric complications): Gaza MoH and UN estimated 5,000+ preventable deaths in 2024 alone. Harm_ly: preventable deaths 5,000 × 30 yrs avg remaining = 150,000; ongoing healthcare deprivation 2.3M × 1.5 yrs × 0.05 = 172,500; total ~320,000.
entries.causation_evidence: WHO 'Attacks on Health Care in Occupied Palestinian Territory 2023-2024'; WHO EMRO situation reports; Gaza Ministry of Health reports; UN OCHA humanitarian updates; Médecins Sans Frontières field reports

entries.description: Long-term cluster munitions contamination in south Lebanon (post-2006 war): HRW and ICRC documented 4.6 million Israeli-fired submunitions across Lebanon, with ~1 million remaining unexploded (UXO); 500+ post-ceasefire casualties through 2018 per Frontiers journal; UXO contaminated ~55 sq km of agricultural land; 30,000+ farming households affected. Harm_ly: post-war deaths ~80 × 35 yrs = 2,800; injuries 420 × 6 yrs disability = 2,520; agricultural land loss 30,000 households × 3 yrs × 0.2 income quality factor = 18,000; long-term land contamination/fear ongoing ~57,000; total ~80,000.
entries.causation_evidence: HRW 'Flooding South Lebanon: Israel's Use of Cluster Munitions in Lebanon' (2008); HRW 'Israel's Use of Cluster Bombs Shows Need for Global Ban' (2008); ICRC cluster munition contamination data; Frontiers in Public Health 'Cluster Munitions: Military Use and Civilian Health Hazards' (2018)

entries.description: Ongoing denial of Palestinian right of return for 5.9M registered refugees (UNRWA 2023): UNGA Resolution 194 (1948) affirmed right of return; ICJ Advisory Opinion (2024) affirmed Israel must allow return of displaced Palestinians. This entry quantifies the ongoing continuous harm of active non-return policy (not attributing the original 1948 displacement in its entirety). Focus: Palestinian refugees in Lebanon (350,000) have no right to work, own property, or access public education—conditions unique to Lebanon among host states; refugees in Jordan, Syria, West Bank, Gaza face lesser but significant restrictions. Harm_ly: Lebanon refugees 350,000 × 25 yrs × 0.04 = 350,000; refugees elsewhere 5.55M × 25 yrs × 0.002 (restricted statehood/residency rights) = 277,500; adjusted total ~700,000.
entries.causation_evidence: UNRWA 'Palestine Refugees' data (2023); UNGA Resolution 194 (1948); ICJ Advisory Opinion (Jul 2024) affirming right of return; UNHCR refugee status reporting; Amnesty International 'Palestinian Refugees Rights' report

entries.description: Killing of journalists and humanitarian workers in Gaza (Oct 2023 - mid-2025): CPJ designated Gaza conflict the deadliest for journalists in CPJ's recorded history; 73+ journalists confirmed killed by CPJ (overall figures including unconfirmed up to 170+); UN OCHA: 400+ humanitarian workers killed including 7 World Central Kitchen staff killed in IDF airstrike Apr 7 2024 (IDF acknowledged as targeting error). Harm_ly: 200 journalists/aid workers killed (conservative CPJ+confirmed aid) × 30 yrs avg remaining = 6,000 direct; systemic suppression of press access and humanitarian operations across ~2.3M people × 1.5 yrs × 0.013 = 45,000; total ~50,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) Gaza conflict database (2024-2025); Reporters Without Borders (RSF) press freedom reports; IDF acknowledgment of World Central Kitchen strike (Apr 2024); UN OCHA humanitarian worker casualty tracking

entries.description: Famine and starvation as method of warfare in Gaza (2023-2025): IPC declared Phase 5 Famine in northern Gaza (May 2024); IPC Phase 4 Emergency across all of Gaza. UNICEF: 300,000 children under 5 at risk of severe acute malnutrition; WHO documented wasting rates among children reaching catastrophic levels. UN Special Rapporteur on Right to Food: starvation deliberately used as method of warfare. Irreversible developmental impacts on children (stunting, brain development impairment). Israeli defence ministry document revealed calorie-count methodology for limiting food supply during blockade (reported 2024). Harm_ly: 300,000 malnourished children × 10 yrs quality-adjusted impairment × 0.08 = 240,000; direct starvation deaths (conservative) 1,500 × 30 yrs = 45,000; adults with severe malnutrition 500,000 × 0.5 × 0.05 = 12,500; adjusted total ~350,000.
entries.causation_evidence: IPC Gaza Food Security Update (May 2024): northern Gaza Phase 5 Famine declaration; UNICEF 'Children Facing Acute Malnutrition in Gaza' reports 2024; WHO EMRO 'Food Insecurity, Starvation and Malnutrition in the Gaza Strip' (2025); UN Special Rapporteur on Right to Food interim report (Oct 2024)

entries.description: Extraction and denial of natural resources in Occupied Palestinian Territories: UNCTAD documented Palestinian economy loses ~$3B/year from restricted economic space plus direct resource extraction by Israeli entities. Key resources: Dead Sea minerals (Israel controls 86% of Dead Sea coastline); Area C stone quarrying (Israeli firms conduct 94% of quarrying); West Bank aquifer over-extraction; Palestinian fishers restricted to 3-6nm (down from Oslo's 20nm), losing 85% of historical fishing grounds. Harm_ly: $3B/yr Palestinian economic loss ÷ $3,000 GDP per capita ≈ 1M person-equivalent-years per year; applying 30-year scope and conservative 1.7% quality weight = 510,000; rounded to 500,000.
entries.causation_evidence: UNCTAD 'The Ongoing Nakba: The Palestinian Economy in the West Bank and Gaza Strip' (2019) and annual OPT reports; B'Tselem 'Dispossession and Exploitation: Israel's Policy in the Jordan Valley and Northern Dead Sea' (2011); Gisha 'Scale of Control: Israel's Continued Responsibility in the Gaza Strip' (fishing zone restrictions); UN OCHA 'Fragmented Lives' 2023

entries.description: Global leadership in life sciences and health-tech: innovations in trauma care, prosthetics, DNA identification, and emergency medicine deployed domestically and internationally
entries.causation_evidence: Israel Life Sciences and Health-Tech Industry Report 2024-25

entries.description: MASHAV international humanitarian assistance: rapid deployment of medical supplies, trauma care training, and crisis response to countries facing disasters
entries.causation_evidence: MASHAV Activity Report 2024 and official statements

entries.description: Netafim drip irrigation technology (founded 1965, Kibbutz Hatzerim): pioneered commercial drip irrigation globally. As of 2025: operating in 110 countries, 8.5M farmers served, 10M+ hectares irrigated, 150B+ drippers produced. Drip irrigation reduces water use 30-50% vs flood methods, increases crop yield 20-50%, enabling food production in otherwise arid or water-scarce regions. Significant food security benefit in sub-Saharan Africa, India, Southeast Asia. Surplus_ly: food security benefit conservatively estimated at 50M smallholder farmers with improved yield/security × 2% quality improvement × 3 yrs attributed scope = 3M LY; but noting this is a commercial technology with many contributing companies, Israel's foundational IP contribution is a fraction: attributed ~300,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Netafim '60 Years' company report (Jul 2025); Netafim Wikipedia page; Stockholm Industry Water Award (2013); UN CEO Water Mandate membership

entries.description: PillCam capsule endoscopy (Given Imaging, Israeli company, 2000): invented first commercial swallowable capsule endoscope enabling non-invasive visualization of small intestine. Now used in 100+ countries; tens of millions of patients examined. Enables earlier detection of small bowel cancer, Crohn's disease, GI bleeding, previously requiring invasive procedures. Meaningful reduction in diagnostic delays and improved long-term outcomes. Surplus_ly: conservative estimate of earlier detection benefit—2,000 lives extended/year × 15 yrs since wide adoption × 5 yrs avg life extension = 150,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Given Imaging company history; Medtronic acquisition documentation (2014); FDA approval records; clinical literature on capsule endoscopy outcomes

entries.description: Abraham Accords diplomatic normalization (Sep-Oct 2020): normalization agreements with UAE, Bahrain, Morocco, Sudan—first Arab-Israeli normalization in 26 years. UAE-Israel bilateral trade grew from near-zero to ~$2.5B/year; reduced probability of state-level military conflict; opened civil aviation links. Combined population of normalizing states ~120M. Surplus_ly: reduced conflict probability over 5 yrs for 120M people × 0.0025% mortality risk reduction = 15,000 lives; plus economic welfare gains 120M × 0.001% quality improvement × 5 yrs = 6,000; adjusted total ~150,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Abraham Accords text (White House, Sep 2020); UAE-Israel trade statistics (2021-2024); Council on Foreign Relations analysis of Abraham Accords outcomes

entries.description: Israel's COVID-19 mass vaccination campaign (Jan-May 2021) provided the first comprehensive real-world evidence of Pfizer BNT162b2 effectiveness. Dagan et al. (NEJM, Feb 2021) confirmed 95.3% vaccine effectiveness against symptomatic COVID-19, immediately informing WHO and global vaccination strategies. Israel vaccinated ~60% of its population by April 2021, months ahead of most countries, generating critical safety and efficacy signals that accelerated confident global rollout. Surplus_ly: if real-world data accelerated global vaccination confidence by 2-4 weeks for ~1B people globally, and 50M lives saved by vaccines (WHO estimate) with 2-week acceleration affecting ~2% of vaccine benefit = 1M lives × 0.5% = 5,000 lives × 20 yrs avg = 100,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Dagan et al., NEJM 'BNT162b2 mRNA Covid-19 Vaccine in a Nationwide Mass Vaccination Setting' (Feb 24, 2021); WHO SAGE documentation citing Israeli data; Nature Medicine 'How Israel's COVID-19 mass vaccination program changed the rules' (2021)

entries.description: Mobileye advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) technology (founded Jerusalem, 1999): developed computer vision and AI-based collision avoidance, lane detection, and pedestrian detection systems now embedded in 125M+ vehicles globally. ADAS technology is estimated to prevent thousands of road traffic fatalities annually. Road traffic kills ~1.35M people/year globally; ADAS systems in equipped vehicles reduce crash probability significantly. Surplus_ly: conservative estimate 2,000 prevented deaths/year × 10 yrs of meaningful deployment × 30 avg remaining life years = 600,000; applying ~33% attribution to Mobileye vs other ADAS providers = 200,000.
entries.causation_evidence: Mobileye company history (founded Jerusalem 1999, Intel acquisition 2017, IPO 2022); Mobileye investor reports on vehicles equipped; WHO Global Road Safety Report (1.35M annual fatalities); NHTSA ADAS effectiveness studies

entries.description: Water desalination and wastewater recycling technology leadership: Israel recycles ~90% of municipal wastewater (world's highest rate, compared to Spain at ~25%, second globally); operates 5 mega-desalination plants providing ~85% of household water; IDE Technologies (Israeli company) built and operates desalination plants in India, California, China. Technology and operational model demonstrably exported. Surplus_ly: global installations by Israeli firms serve ~15M people internationally; water security improvement quality factor 0.01 × 15M × 5 yrs weighted contribution = 750,000; attributing 10% of benefit to Israeli IP/operational expertise = 75,000; rounded to 100,000.
entries.causation_evidence: IDE Technologies project portfolio; Israel Water Authority annual reports; OECD 'Water Reuse' report citing Israel's 90% recycling rate; various media coverage of IDE plants in India (Nemmeli, Minjur) and California

entries.description: Agricultural biotechnology contributions: Hebrew University of Jerusalem researchers developed the modern cherry tomato variety (1970s) now a global staple crop; various disease-resistant crop varieties for challenging climates. Distinct from MASHAV training (isr_surplus_002) and drip irrigation (isr_surplus_003), this entry covers specific IP and crop variety contributions used by millions of farmers globally. Surplus_ly: cherry tomato and associated varieties grown across 500,000 ha globally; improved nutrition and farmer income ~80,000 person-years benefit attributed to Israeli research origin.
entries.causation_evidence: Hebrew University of Jerusalem publications on cherry tomato breeding program (Eshed & Zamir, 1994); FAO crop statistics on cherry tomato production; academic literature on Israeli contributions to crop genetics
